plan_metadata.name: Feature Verify Metadata Zip File Fetch And Cache For RC Version

steps.description: Click the language selection button (displaying a British flag) at the top-right corner of the VS Code welcome/sign-in dialog.
Click the "Explorer" icon in the left sidebar of Visual Studio Code to open the file navigation panel.
Type the text 'm365 agents toolkit' into the search input field in the Extensions: Marketplace panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension entry in the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace panel after searching for "m365 agents toolkit".
Click the "Switch to Pre-release Version" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension details pane within the Visual Studio Code Marketplace tab.
@assertion "Switch to Release Version" exists.
Use keyboard shortcut **Ctrl+Shift+~** in the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace to open a new integrated terminal while the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension details panel is in focus.
Type text `rm -rf ~/.fx/` into the integrated Terminal at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code interface, within a workspace session running on a Bash shell.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal to execute the command `rm -rf ~/.fx/`.
Click the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension entry in the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace sidebar to select and display its details.
Use keyboard shortcut ctrl+shift+~ to open a new terminal in the Visual Studio Code workspace interface.
Type the text 'ls ~/.fx/ui/' into the terminal input field in the Terminal panel of Visual Studio Code.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal to execute the command `ls ~/.fx/ui/`.
@assertion "wizardNode.json" exists.
Type the command `cat ~/.fx/ui/wizardNode.json` into the integrated Terminal input field in Visual Studio Code, within the workspace environment.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal after entering the command `cat ~/.fx/ui/wizardNode.json`.
@assertion "office-meta-os-type" exists.
Type the text `ls ~/.fx/` into the integrated terminal input of Visual Studio Code, within the bottom panel labeled "TERMINAL" set to the "bash" shell.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code Terminal after typing "ls ~/.fx/" to execute the command and display directory contents.
@assertion "template-version.txt" exists,
Type the text 'cat ~/.fx/template-version.txt' into the active input field of the integrated Terminal panel within Visual Studio Code, under the 'bash' shell session.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal (bash) after typing the command `cat ~/.fx/template-version.txt`.
@assertion "0.0.0-rc" exists.
Click the "Build a Declarative Agent" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar of the Visual Studio Code welcome interface.

Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension entry in the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace sidebar after searching for "m365 agents toolkit." The red crosshair marks the extension's listing, which features its icon, publisher (Microsoft), and install button.
Click the "Switch to Release Version" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension details panel within the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace interface.
Click the "Restart Extensions" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension detail pane within the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace interface.

Use keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+~ in Visual Studio Code to open a new terminal tab (specifically the "bash" shell), while viewing the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension details in the Extensions Marketplace panel.
Type the command 'ls ~/.fx/ui' into the integrated bash terminal in Visual Studio Code, located within the workspace interface.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code terminal after typing the command `ls ~/.fx/ui` to execute the directory listing.
@assertion "wizardNode.json" exists
@assertion "office-meta-os-type" exists.
Type the text 'ls ~/.fx/' into the VS Code integrated terminal (bash) input at the bottom of the interface.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal to execute the command `ls ~/.fx/`.
@assertion "template-version.txt" exists.
Type the text 'cat ~/.fx/template-version.txt' into the integrated Terminal input field in Visual Studio Code, with the terminal set to 'bash' at the bottom panel of the workspace interface.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal after typing `cat ~/.fx/template-version.txt` to execute the command.
@assertion "0.0.0-rc" exists.
